items.name: Biggoron's Sword
items.effect: A legendary greatsword with an exceptionally sharp cutting edge; high attack and very high durability.
items.where: Chest in the Akkala House of Bones in the Depths beneath Skull Lake (via Skull Lake Chasm), after defeating a Stalnox.

items.name: Boat Oar
items.effect: A sturdy paddle usable for self-defense; very low power.
items.where: East Necluda and along the Necluda Sea; found near docks and boats.

items.name: Bokoblin Arm
items.effect: A skeletal Bokoblin arm that keeps twitching; high power but breaks very quickly. Being bone, it floats on water and won't attract lightning.
items.where: Dropped by Stalkoblins (skeletal Bokoblins) that rise at night across Hyrule and in the Depths.

items.name: Boomerang
items.effect: Returns to Link after being thrown; can hit at range.
items.where: Lanayru Wetlands (e.g. near Lizalfos/Bokoblin camps by large logs).

items.name: Boomerang+
items.effect: Pristine Boomerang; returns when thrown, with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Boulder Breaker
items.effect: The Goron Champion's weapon — requires great strength to wield and excels at mining ore deposits (Demolisher).
items.where: Reward for the side quest "Soul of the Gorons" at the Goron City forge after restoring Yunobo (bring a Cobble Crusher, 3 Diamonds, 5 Flint).

items.name: Cobble Crusher
items.effect: A heavy Goron hammer that smashes rocks and ore deposits with ease (Demolisher).
items.where: Eldin Canyon and the Canyon Depths; found around Goron territory.

items.name: Decayed Master Sword
items.effect: The Master Sword with its sacred power diminished by the gloom; the weakest weapon in the game and it does not restore itself when depleted. Fuse cannot be used on it.
items.where: Your starting weapon on the Great Sky Island (Room of Awakening) — used to cut vines; replaced once you find real weapons.

items.name: Dusk Claymore
items.effect: An ancient blade that shines with a holy luster; high attack with strong durability.
items.where: Chest by the monolith inside the risen structure during the Thyphlo Ruins / "Camera Work in the Depths" investigation (also via Ganondorf amiibo).

items.name: Eightfold Blade
items.effect: Improved Sneakstrike skill — bonus damage on stealth attacks.
items.where: Around Kakariko Village and the Gerudo Highlands (Sheikah-made).

items.name: Eightfold Blade+
items.effect: Pristine Eightfold Blade; Improved Sneakstrike with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Eightfold Longblade
items.effect: A Sheikah longblade that cleaves the wind and creates a vacuum when wielded skillfully (Wind Razor).
items.where: Hyrule Ridge and Gerudo Highlands; carried by Yiga Clan members.

items.name: Farmer's Pitchfork
items.effect: A simple farming tool — no special property; decent reach for an early-game polearm.
items.where: Hyrule Field and West Necluda farms (around Hateno, stables, and fields).

items.name: Farming Hoe
items.effect: A farming tool with no combat bonus; weak and fragile.
items.where: Hyrule Field and West Necluda; found around farms and stables.

items.name: Feathered Edge
items.effect: Wind Burst skill — produces a gust of wind when swung.
items.where: Hebra Mountains and Tabantha Frontier (Rito-made).

items.name: Feathered Edge+
items.effect: Pristine Feathered Edge; Wind Burst with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Feathered Spear
items.effect: A Rito-made spear with Wind Burst — its attacks can kick up a gust of wind.
items.where: Hebra Mountains and Tabantha Frontier (Rito Village region; e.g. chest from an Ice Like in a cave near Pikida Stonegrove).

items.name: Fierce Deity Sword
items.effect: A great double-helix blade that slashes wildly in battle as if possessed.
items.where: Cephla Lake Cave as part of Misko's treasure (also via the Majora's Mask Link / Fierce Deity amiibo).

items.name: Fishing Harpoon
items.effect: A fisher's harpoon — no special property; basic reach weapon.
items.where: Lurelin Village (by the cooking campfire and around the docks/houses).

items.name: Forest Dweller's Spear
items.effect: Has Fuse Recycling — one-time-use fused materials can be reused, so the fuse part survives the weapon breaking.
items.where: Great Hyrule Forest / Korok Forest, Eldin Mountains, and Depths Shadow-Soldier drops (Korok-made gear).

items.name: Forest Dweller's Sword
items.effect: Fuse Recycling skill — the fused material can be recovered when the weapon breaks.
items.where: Eldin Mountains and the Great Hyrule Forest (Korok-made).

items.name: Forest Dweller's Sword+
items.effect: Pristine Forest Dweller's Sword; Fuse Recycling with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Gerudo Claymore
items.effect: A Gerudo-made claymore whose power is greatly enhanced when a material is fused to it (Strong Fusion).
items.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands; carried by Gerudo soldiers.

items.name: Gerudo Scimitar
items.effect: Strong Fusions skill — maximizes the power of the fused material.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and around Gerudo Town.

items.name: Gerudo Scimitar+
items.effect: Pristine Gerudo Scimitar; Strong Fusions with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Gerudo Spear
items.effect: Has Strong Fusion — doubles the fuse attack power of the attached material, making it excellent for fuse builds (but adds less durability).
items.where: Gerudo Desert / Gerudo Highlands (near Gerudo Town, enemy camps and chests).

items.name: Giant Boomerang
items.effect: An oversized Rito boomerang that can be thrown and returns to the wielder.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon; carried by enemies near Rito territory.

items.name: Gloom Club
items.effect: A massive gloom-wreathed club; highest base power among two-handers but its hits inflict gloom on Link (Gloom Toll).
items.where: Dropped by a Phantom Ganon that spawns after defeating Gloom Hands (e.g. SE of Birida Lookout near Mayatat Shrine, Gerudo Desert).

items.name: Gloom Spear
items.effect: Has Gloom Toll — extremely high base attack, but each hit locks (drains) Link's max hearts; hearts recover on the Surface/Sky. A unique Phantom Ganon weapon.
items.where: The Depths / Surface — dropped by a Phantom Ganon (spawned after defeating Gloom Hands).

items.name: Gloom Sword
items.effect: The strongest unfused one-handed weapon (power 41), but using it inflicts gloom damage on Link (never directly fatal). Low durability.
items.where: Dropped by Phantom Ganon (which appears after you defeat the Gloom Hands it emerges from), found near chasms/gloom across the surface.

items.name: Gnarled Long Stick
items.effect: A knobby branch — no special property, but surprisingly high attack for a wooden stick.
items.where: Gerudo Desert (forested oases and tree clusters).

items.name: Gnarled Thick Stick
items.effect: A knotted heavy branch with unusually high attack power for a wooden weapon.
items.where: East Necluda and the Gerudo Highlands; forested areas.

items.name: Gnarled Wooden Stick
items.effect: A toughened stick with far greater attack power than ordinary sticks.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and around Mount Lanayru.

items.name: Knight's Broadsword
items.effect: Desperate Strength skill — attack power rises when Link is low on health.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and Hyrule Castle.

items.name: Knight's Broadsword+
items.effect: Pristine Knight's Broadsword; Desperate Strength with much higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Knight's Claymore
items.effect: A knight's claymore that yields powerful attacks when you're down to your last heart (Desperate Strength).
items.where: Hyrule Field and Gerudo Desert; on stronger enemies (e.g. a water chest in Popla Foothills North Cave).

items.name: Knight's Halberd
items.effect: Has Desperate Strength — deals double damage while Link is down to one heart or less.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and Faron Grasslands (mid-game enemy camps and ruins).

items.name: Lightscale Trident
items.effect: Mipha's signature trident — has Water Warrior, doubling attack while wet; very high durability.
items.where: Zora's Domain — reforged by Dento in the 'Glory of the Zora' side quest (bring a Zora Spear, 3 Diamonds, and 5 Flint) after the Sidon main quest.

items.name: Lizal Boomerang
items.effect: A Lizalfos-made boomerang that returns when thrown.
items.where: Hebra Mountains and around Mount Lanayru; dropped by Lizalfos.

items.name: Lizalfos Arm
items.effect: A skeletal Lizalfos arm; high power but extremely brittle. Bone, so it floats and won't attract lightning.
items.where: Dropped by Stalizalfos (skeletal Lizalfos) at night and in the Depths.

items.name: Long Stick
items.effect: A plain tree-branch — no special property; its straight shape makes it a serviceable lunging weapon.
items.where: Tabantha Frontier and Hebra Mountains (forested areas).

items.name: Magic Rod
items.effect: Low melee damage, but fusing an elemental gem turns it into an elemental rod (Ruby=fire, Sapphire=ice, Topaz=lightning) that fires elemental projectiles.
items.where: Carried (pre-fused) by higher-level Wizzrobes, found in some shrine chests, and buyable from Depths Bargainer Statues for 100 Poes.

items.name: Magic Scepter
items.effect: A magical rod that awakens the latent power of a gem fused to it.
items.where: Central Hyrule Depths and Mount Lanayru Depths; held by Wizzrobe-type foes / found in the Depths.

items.name: Magic Staff
items.effect: An ancient magician's rod — no special property; its charge attack fires a forward stream of shots. Flavorful low-tier polearm.
items.where: Eldin Mountains and North Gerudo Sky Archipelago (sky islands, shrine chests; also buyable from a Depths Bargainer Statue).

items.name: Master Sword
items.effect: The legendary blade that seals the darkness; it never permanently breaks (its energy recharges after running out). Damage doubles to 60 against Ganondorf (with effectively infinite durability) and rises ~1.5x (to ~45) against gloom-spawned foes.
items.where: Pull it from the Light Dragon's head once you have enough hearts/stamina (tied to recovering the Master Sword's sacred power).

items.name: Mighty Zonaite Longsword
items.effect: The strongest Zonaite two-hander, greatly increasing attack power (Zonaite-Powered).
items.where: Sky islands and the Depths; carried by tougher Soldier Constructs.

items.name: Mighty Zonaite Spear
items.effect: The top zonaite spear — resonates with attached Zonai devices/materials to raise its attack power; best of the Zonai polearms.
items.where: Sky archipelagos and the Depths (dropped by higher-tier Soldier Constructs; chests at North Lomei Labyrinth, Lanayru Sky Archipelago).

items.name: Mighty Zonaite Sword
items.effect: Zonaite-Powered skill (top tier) — the greatest Zonai-device boost of the Zonaite swords.
items.where: Sky islands and the Depths; wielded by Soldier/Captain Construct IV.

items.name: Moblin Arm
items.effect: A torn-off Moblin arm that keeps swinging; high power but extremely brittle.
items.where: Great Hyrule Forest and the Deep Akkala Depths; from Moblins/Gloom Spawn.

items.name: Royal Broadsword
items.effect: Improved Flurry Rush skill — flurry-rush attacks deal extra damage.
items.where: Hyrule Castle and the Gerudo Highlands Depths.

items.name: Royal Broadsword+
items.effect: Pristine Royal Broadsword; Improved Flurry Rush with much higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Royal Claymore
items.effect: A claymore of the royal guard that increases the power of flurry rush during a perfect dodge.
items.where: Great Plateau and Hyrule Castle (e.g. a 1F pillar top and the Lookout Landing well).

items.name: Royal Guard's Claymore
items.effect: Hits with massive power for its short life — high attack but breaks quickly (Breaking Point).
items.where: Hyrule Castle (notably Zelda's Room, B1).

items.name: Royal Guard's Spear
items.effect: Has Breaking Point — displayed power jumps sharply (to about 32) when the weapon is nearly broken; very low durability.
items.where: Hyrule Castle (Sanctum / interior storage and lockup rooms).

items.name: Royal Guard's Sword
items.effect: Breaking Point skill — deals massive bonus damage when its durability is nearly spent.
items.where: Hyrule Castle (e.g. the Sanctum and Princess Zelda's Study).

items.name: Royal Guard's Sword+
items.effect: Pristine Royal Guard's Sword; Breaking Point with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Royal Halberd
items.effect: Has Improved Flurry Rush — deals extra damage during a flurry rush after a perfect dodge. A high-end royal polearm.
items.where: Hyrule Castle and East Necluda (castle rooftops/armories and chests).

items.name: Rusty Broadsword
items.effect: A corroded sword that is quick to break; a Rock Octorok can polish it into a Royal/Knight's-tier variant.
items.where: Hyrule Field and the East Necluda Depths.

items.name: Rusty Claymore
items.effect: A corroded claymore with very low durability; fuse a material to make it usable.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon Depths; dropped by Soldier Constructs and found in ruins.

items.name: Rusty Halberd
items.effect: A corroded old polearm — no special property; weak and fragile. Fuse a part or burn off the rust to refresh it into a better weapon.
items.where: Hyrule Field and East Necluda, near ruins, especially when wet or raining.

items.name: Scimitar of the Seven
items.effect: Urbosa's heirloom blade; Strong Fusions skill and the single most durable one-handed weapon (gets the full +25 durability from fusion).
items.where: Reward from the Pride of the Gerudo side quest in Gerudo Town (gather diamonds for Isha).

items.name: Sea-Breeze Boomerang
items.effect: A sturdy boomerang that returns when thrown; high power for its class.
items.where: Chest in Hebra Canyon Mine (the Depths, below Hebra Peak); also via Toon Link amiibo, then re-buyable from any Depths Bargainer Statue for 100 Poes.

items.name: Soldier's Broadsword
items.effect: Quick Charge skill — performs charged spin attacks faster than normal.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon; carried by Hyrulean soldier remains.

items.name: Soldier's Broadsword+
items.effect: Pristine Soldier's Broadsword; Quick Charge with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Soldier's Claymore
items.effect: A lightweight military claymore that consumes less stamina during charged attacks.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon; carried by Soldier Constructs (a copy sits in the Hyrule Castle training room).

items.name: Soldier's Spear
items.effect: Has Quick Charge — light enough that charged spin attacks build up noticeably faster.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon (Soldier Construct camps and ruins; e.g. Lookout Landing's hidden passage).

items.name: Soup Ladle
items.effect: A kitchen implement repurposed as a weapon; low power and durability.
items.where: Stables and cooking areas around Hyrule Field and West Necluda.

items.name: Strong Zonaite Longsword
items.effect: A stronger Zonaite two-hander with enhanced Zonai-device resonance (Zonaite-Powered).
items.where: Lanayru Sky Archipelago and Thunderhead Isles; from sky Constructs.

items.name: Strong Zonaite Spear
items.effect: A stronger zonaite spear — resonates with attached Zonai devices/materials to raise its attack power.
items.where: Sky islands and the Depths (higher-tier Soldier Construct drops and chests; e.g. North Lomei Labyrinth, Hebra).

items.name: Strong Zonaite Sword
items.effect: Zonaite-Powered skill (middle tier) — greater Zonai-device boost than the basic Zonaite Sword.
items.where: Sky islands and the Depths; dropped by stronger Constructs.

items.name: Sturdy Long Stick
items.effect: Has Extra Durable — cut from the hardest part of the tree, so it lasts noticeably longer than a normal stick.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and East Necluda (forest groves and tree clusters).

items.name: Sturdy Thick Stick
items.effect: A surprisingly tough wooden club with high durability for its appearance (Extra Durable).
items.where: Hyrule Field and Gerudo Desert; forested areas.

items.name: Sturdy Wooden Stick
items.effect: Extra Durable skill — unusually high durability for a stick.
items.where: Hyrule Field and East Necluda.

items.name: Sword of the Hero
items.effect: A blade modeled on the original 1986 hero's sword; no special skill but solid, durable stats. Conducts electricity.
items.where: Scan the 8-Bit Link (30th Anniversary) amiibo, OR find it in the Depths in a hollow tree stump at Dalite Grove — afterwards re-buyable from any Bargainer Statue for 100 Poes.

items.name: Thick Stick
items.effect: A heavy wooden bat; can still knock a shield out of an enemy's hands.
items.where: Hyrule Field and the Hebra Mountains; common forest pickup.

items.name: Throwing Spear
items.effect: Has Long Throw — built to be hurled, it flies farther when thrown.
items.where: Central Hyrule Depths (carried by enemies and found in Depths camps).

items.name: Torch
items.effect: Stays lit when ignited; mainly a light source and fire-spreader rather than a weapon.
items.where: Found at enemy camps, stables, and around Hyrule Field and Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Traveler's Claymore
items.effect: A common claymore that can still knock a shield right out of an enemy's hands.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Gerudo Desert; common Bokoblin/Hyrule-overworld weapon (e.g. Sahasra Slope Cave ground).

items.name: Traveler's Spear
items.effect: A wanderer's spear — no special property; light, basic reach weapon.
items.where: Death Mountain / Eldin and Gerudo Highlands (traveler camps and roadsides).

items.name: Traveler's Sword
items.effect: A common, balanced sword with no special skill.
items.where: Hyrule Field and the Central Hyrule Depths.

items.name: Traveler's Sword+
items.effect: The pristine Traveler's Sword with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine chests/weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Tree Branch
items.effect: The weakest common weapon; well-balanced but breaks almost instantly. Burns if ignited.
items.where: Lying near trees and bushes across the Great Sky Island and Hyrule Field.

items.name: White Sword of the Sky
items.effect: The sky hero's white blade; high attack and very durable, no special skill. Conducts electricity.
items.where: Reward from the Mother Goddess Statue at the Forgotten Temple after completing the three Goddess Statue side quests (Wisdom/Courage/Power).

items.name: Wooden Mop
items.effect: A janitor's mop pressed into service — no special property; weak and fragile, useful mainly as an early fuse handle.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon (leaning in stables, houses, and shacks); common around Lookout Landing.

items.name: Wooden Stick
items.effect: A simple stick with good balance; burns if set alight.
items.where: Hyrule Field and the Central Hyrule Depths.

items.name: Zonaite Longsword
items.effect: A Zonaite two-hander that resonates with attached Zonai devices to slightly increase attack power (Zonaite-Powered).
items.where: Lanayru Sky Archipelago and Necluda Sky Archipelago; carried by Soldier Constructs in the sky.

items.name: Zonaite Spear
items.effect: A Zonai spear of zonaite that resonates with attached Zonai devices/materials to raise its attack power.
items.where: Sky islands and the Depths (Captain/Soldier Construct drops, Zonaite Forge Island, chests).

items.name: Zonaite Sword
items.effect: Zonaite-Powered skill — boosts the attack of fused Zonai devices.
items.where: Sky islands; dropped by Soldier/Captain Constructs.

items.name: Zora Longsword
items.effect: A Zora blade whose attack power rises while Link is wet (Water Warrior).
items.where: Lanayru Great Spring / Zora's Domain area.

items.name: Zora Spear
items.effect: Has Water Warrior — deals extra damage while wet (in rain or water).
items.where: Lanayru Wetlands and Upland Zorana (Zora's Domain region; chests, Lizalfos, Like Likes).

items.name: Zora Sword
items.effect: Water Warrior skill — attack power increases while Link is wet or in the rain.
items.where: Zora's Domain and the Lanayru Great Spring.

items.name: Zora Sword+
items.effect: Pristine Zora Sword; Water Warrior with higher attack and durability.
items.where: Pristine weapon stands in the Depths.

items.name: Boko Bow
items.effect: Crude Bokoblin bow; single shot, very low durability.
items.where: Bokoblin drop — Hyrule Field, Central Hyrule Depths

items.name: Construct Bow
items.effect: The basic Soldier Construct bow; single shot.
items.where: Soldier Construct drop — South Lanayru Sky Archipelago, North Gerudo Sky Archipelago

items.name: Demon King's Bow
items.effect: Power scales with Link's hearts (+2 per Heart Container, base 12, capped at 60 at 30 hearts); single shot, comes pre-broken-in. Cannot be upgraded by Rock Octoroks.
items.where: Always dropped by Phantom Ganon (defeat the Gloom Hands first to spawn it) — random Depths/surface spawns

items.name: Dragonbone Boko Bow
items.effect: Strongest Bokoblin bow; single shot, high power for its family.
items.where: Bokoblin drop — Gerudo Highlands, Gerudo Highlands Depths

items.name: Duplex Bow
items.effect: Multishot: fires 2 arrows at once (14x2); low durability.
items.where: Hyrule Field, Gerudo Desert Depths

items.name: Dusk Bow
items.effect: A royal single-shot bow with long range; respawns every Blood Moon if lost or broken.
items.where: Alcove in the tallest central steeple of Hyrule Castle, directly above the Sanctum

items.name: Falcon Bow
items.effect: A fast-drawing Rito bow; single shot with quick fire rate.
items.where: Tabantha Frontier, Hebra Mountains

items.name: Forest Dweller's Bow
items.effect: Multishot: fires 3 arrows at once (15x3); a Korok-made wooden bow.
items.where: Great Hyrule Forest

items.name: Gerudo Bow
items.effect: A long-range Gerudo hunting bow; single shot with good range.
items.where: Gerudo Desert, Gerudo Highlands

items.name: Great Eagle Bow
items.effect: Multishot: fires 3 arrows in a spread (28x3); the Rito Champion Revali's bow, very sturdy. Cannot take modifiers.
items.where: Crafted by Teba in Rito Village after Tulin's quest (Swallow Bow + 3 Diamonds + 5 Wood); if broken, Harth reforges it for a Swallow Bow + 1 Diamond + 5 Wood

items.name: Knight's Bow
items.effect: A reliable Hylian knight's single-shot bow with high durability.
items.where: Gerudo Desert, Akkala Highlands

items.name: Lizal Bow
items.effect: Basic Lizalfos bow; single shot.
items.where: Lizalfos drop — Lanayru Wetlands, Gerudo Desert Depths

items.name: Lynel Bow
items.effect: Multishot: fires 3 arrows at once (10x3) from a single arrow.
items.where: Lynel drop — East Necluda, Deep Akkala

items.name: Mighty Construct Bow
items.effect: Top-tier Soldier Construct bow; single shot, strong power.
items.where: Soldier Construct drop — Lomei Labyrinth Island, Thyphlo Ruins

items.name: Mighty Lynel Bow
items.effect: Multishot: fires 3 arrows at once (20x3).
items.where: Lynel drop — Lanayru Great Spring, Akkala Highlands Depths

items.name: Old Wooden Bow
items.effect: A flimsy starter bow that fires a single arrow; lowest tier, breaks quickly.
items.where: Great Sky Island (the game's opening area)

items.name: Phrenic Bow
items.effect: Single shot but very high durability for its power; a Sheikah bow that lets you target distant foes as easily as near ones.
items.where: East Necluda, West Necluda

items.name: Royal Bow
items.effect: A sturdy single-shot royal bow with excellent durability.
items.where: Hyrule Field, Central Hyrule Depths

items.name: Royal Guard's Bow
items.effect: Highest base-power bow in the game with a very fast (rapid) fire rate, but low durability.
items.where: On the royal desk in Princess Zelda's study, Hyrule Castle

items.name: Savage Lynel Bow
items.effect: Multishot: fires 3 arrows at once (32x3); the strongest Lynel bow.
items.where: White-maned/Silver Lynel drop — Gerudo Highlands, Lanayru Wetlands Depths

items.name: Soldier's Bow
items.effect: A standard Hylian soldier's single-shot bow; reliable mid-tier.
items.where: Hyrule Field, Hebra Mountains

items.name: Spiked Boko Bow
items.effect: Upgraded Bokoblin bow; single shot, modest power.
items.where: Bokoblin drop — Hyrule Field, Gerudo Highlands Depths

items.name: Steel Lizal Bow
items.effect: Strongest Lizalfos bow; single shot, high power and durability.
items.where: Lizalfos drop — Gerudo Highlands Depths, Central Hyrule Depths

items.name: Strengthened Lizal Bow
items.effect: Mid Lizalfos bow; single shot.
items.where: Lizalfos drop — Gerudo Desert, Akkala Highlands Depths

items.name: Strong Construct Bow
items.effect: Mid-tier Soldier Construct bow; single shot.
items.where: Soldier Construct drop — Rising Island Chain, Death Mountain Depths

items.name: Swallow Bow
items.effect: A light Rito-style single-shot bow valued for fast draw speed.
items.where: Hebra Mountains, Tabantha Frontier

items.name: Traveler's Bow
items.effect: A common traveler's single-shot bow, slightly sturdier than the wooden bows.
items.where: Hyrule Field, Central Hyrule Depths

items.name: Wooden Bow
items.effect: A basic single-shot bow; weakest of the surface bows.
items.where: Hyrule Field, South Lanayru Sky Archipelago

items.name: Zonaite Bow
items.effect: A Zonai bow that draws on your Energy Cell — drains energy as you hold a shot, and arrows fly farther the longer you charge.
items.where: Chest in Kikakin Shrine (Depths)

items.name: Zora Bow
items.effect: A Zora-crafted single-shot bow; solid mid-tier power and durability.
items.where: Lanayru Great Spring (Zora's Domain region)

items.name: Boko Shield
items.effect: The basic wooden Bokoblin shield — very fragile and flammable.
items.where: Dropped by red Bokoblins (e.g., Hyrule Field, Hebra Mountains).

items.name: Daybreaker
items.effect: A golden shield cherished by the Gerudo Champion Urbosa — lightweight yet very durable for its guard value. One-of-a-kind (remade by Buliara for 1 Diamond + 5 Flint).
items.where: Obtained through the story in Gerudo Town.

items.name: Dragonbone Boko Shield
items.effect: The strongest Bokoblin shield, made from bone — low durability.
items.where: Dropped by black/silver Bokoblins (e.g., Gerudo Highlands, Gerudo Desert).

items.name: Emblazoned Shield
items.effect: A decorated wooden shield — burns if exposed to fire.
items.where: West Necluda and East Necluda.

items.name: Fisherman's Shield
items.effect: A simple wooden shield used by Lurelin fishers — flammable.
items.where: Lurelin Village (Necluda coast).

items.name: Forest Dweller's Shield
items.effect: A wooden Korok-made shield — burns if exposed to fire.
items.where: Great Hyrule Forest (around the Korok Forest / Lost Woods).

items.name: Gerudo Shield
items.effect: A standard Gerudo soldier's shield with balanced stats.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands (enemy camps).

items.name: Hunter's Shield
items.effect: A light wooden hunter's shield — flammable.
items.where: Tabantha Frontier and Hebra Mountains.

items.name: Hylian Shield
items.effect: The single best shield in the game by far — highest base guard and the highest durability of any shield.
items.where: Hyrule Castle: cross the moat to Hyrule Castle Docks, climb past the Gloom Hands and light the giant brazier to open the door to the chest. If it breaks, Cece in Hateno Village will sell a replacement for 3,000 Rupees.

items.name: Kite Shield
items.effect: A light, tall shield favored by Rito-region travelers.
items.where: Hebra Mountains and the Tabantha Frontier.

items.name: Knight's Shield
items.effect: A solid mid-tier defensive shield once carried by Hyrulean knights.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and the Tabantha Frontier Depths (enemy camps / Depths).

items.name: Lizal Shield
items.effect: The basic Lizalfos shield; lightweight but fragile.
items.where: Dropped by Lizalfos in Lanayru Great Spring and the Faron Sea.

items.name: Lynel Shield
items.effect: The basic Lynel shield; can be used to shield-bash.
items.where: Dropped by red-maned Lynels (e.g., Hyrule Field, Central Hyrule Depths).

items.name: Mighty Lynel Shield
items.effect: A strong Lynel shield that can be used to shield-bash.
items.where: Dropped by blue-maned Lynels (e.g., Faron Grasslands, Eldin Canyon Depths).

items.name: Mighty Zonaite Shield
items.effect: A Zonai-tech shield: a fused Zonai device draws on the shield's stored energy so it consumes less battery to operate. Immune to fire and lightning (not wood or metal).
items.where: Rarely on the Sky Islands and, most commonly, in the Depths beneath Hyrule.

items.name: Old Wooden Shield
items.effect: A weathered wooden shield — flammable.
items.where: Great Sky Island (the tutorial area) and the Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Pot Lid
items.effect: A cooking-pot lid used as a makeshift shield — lowest guard, but a fun perfect-guard / parry tool.
items.where: Hyrule Field and East Necluda (found near houses, stables, and cooking areas).

items.name: Radiant Shield
items.effect: A decorated Gerudo shield with good guard and durability.
items.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands (enemy camps).

items.name: Reinforced Lizal Shield
items.effect: A reinforced Lizalfos shield, sturdier than the basic version.
items.where: Dropped by Lizalfos in the Tabantha Tundra and the Abandoned Kakariko Mine.

items.name: Royal Guard's Shield
items.effect: Very high guard but extremely fragile — a Hyrule Castle heirloom that breaks after only a few hits.
items.where: Hyrule Castle: from the Inner Sanctum, take the staircase to the right of the throne.

items.name: Royal Shield
items.effect: A high-guard royal shield with respectable durability.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Gerudo Highlands Depths (enemy camps / Depths).

items.name: Rusty Shield
items.effect: A corroded old knight's shield; a Rock Octorok can restore it, spitting it back out as a random higher-tier shield.
items.where: Hyrule Field and East Necluda (often found embedded in the ground).

items.name: Savage Lynel Shield
items.effect: The strongest non-royal/non-Hylian shield; like all Lynel shields it can be used to shield-bash.
items.where: Dropped by silver and white-maned Lynels (e.g., Akkala Highlands and the Central Hyrule Depths).

items.name: Sea-Breeze Shield
items.effect: Said to have been the favorite of a hero who sailed the open seas (a Wind Waker nod); high guard and durability. One-of-a-kind.
items.where: In the Depths in Tabahl Grove (east of the Sekioam Lightroot) — enter via the Lanayru Wetlands Chasm north of Jonsau Shrine and head to the hollow tree stump.

items.name: Shield of the Mind's Eye
items.effect: A semi-transparent shield — it is nearly invisible when raised.
items.where: East Necluda and West Necluda.

items.name: Soldier's Shield
items.effect: A standard Hyrulean soldier's shield with balanced low stats.
items.where: Hyrule Field and Eldin Canyon (enemy camps / ruins).

items.name: Spiked Boko Shield
items.effect: A spiked Bokoblin shield, a step up from the plain Boko Shield.
items.where: Dropped by blue/black Bokoblins (e.g., Hyrule Field, East Necluda).

items.name: Steel Lizal Shield
items.effect: The sturdiest Lizalfos shield, made of metal — conducts electricity, so beware shocks in storms.
items.where: Dropped by Lizalfos in Gerudo Desert and around Mount Lanayru.

items.name: Strong Zonaite Shield
items.effect: A Zonai-tech shield: a fused Zonai device consumes less energy than normal when attached.
items.where: Lanayru Sky Archipelago and Lomei Labyrinth Island (Sky), and the Depths.

items.name: Traveler's Shield
items.effect: A common traveler's shield of animal hide and wood with very low guard.
items.where: Hyrule Field and the Eldin Mountains (early-game pickups).

items.name: Wooden Shield
items.effect: A plain wooden shield — burns if exposed to fire.
items.where: Hyrule Field and the Hebra Mountains.

items.name: Zonaite Shield
items.effect: The basic Zonai-tech shield: a fused Zonai device consumes less energy when used.
items.where: Necluda Archipelago and Eldin Sky Archipelago (Sky Islands), and the Depths.

items.name: Zora Shield
items.effect: A Zora-crafted shield; conducts electricity, so beware shocks.
items.where: Lanayru Great Spring (Zora's Domain) and its Sky Archipelago.

items.name: Amber Earrings
items.effect: No special effect; valued for high base defense that climbs as you upgrade with Amber. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Bought at Starlight Memories jewelry shop, Gerudo Town (rupees + Amber).

items.name: Ancient Hero's Aspect
items.effect: Highest base defense of any body piece; a single all-slots item whose 2-star bonus is Master Sword Beam Up (stronger Master Sword beams).
items.where: Reward for completing all 152 shrines — chest behind the Goddess Statue at the Temple of Time.

items.name: Ancient Hero's Headgear
items.effect: Fills all three armor slots at once (no body/leg piece); on its own grants the Master Sword Beam Up bonus (boosts Master Sword beam damage). Single-piece set; cannot be upgraded.
items.where: Chest behind the Goddess Statue in the Temple of Time on Great Sky Island, after completing all 152 Shrines.

items.name: Ancient Hero's Trousers
items.effect: No special property; part of the Ancient Hero's Aspect (a single full-body costume counted across all three slots), the highest base-defense armor in the game.
items.where: Reward in the Temple of Time on the Great Sky Island after completing all 152 shrines.

items.name: Archaic Legwear
items.effect: No special property and no set bonus; the starting leg wear.
items.where: Chest near the exit of the Room of Awakening on the Great Sky Island (start of the game).

items.name: Archaic Tunic
items.effect: No effect; part of the 2-piece Archaic starter set, which has no set bonus and cannot be upgraded by a Great Fairy.
items.where: Chest in Pondside Cave in the southwest of the Great Sky Island (the starting tutorial area).

items.name: Archaic Warm Greaves
items.effect: Cold Resistance (keeps Link warm in cold areas); no set bonus.
items.where: Chest inside a tree-like cave near Gutanbac Shrine on the Great Sky Island.

items.name: Barbarian Armor
items.effect: Attack Up; full Barbarian set's 2-star bonus is Charge Atk. Stamina Up (less stamina used by charged attacks).
items.where: Chest inside Crenel Hills Cave.

items.name: Barbarian Helm
items.effect: Attack Up. Part of the Barbarian set — set bonus 'Charge Atk. Stamina Up'.
items.where: Chest in Robred Dropoff Cave, East Necluda.

items.name: Barbarian Leg Wraps
items.effect: Attack Up; the Barbarian set bonus 'Charge Attack Stamina Up' (less stamina for charged attacks) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Burn the ice then open the chest in Walnot Mountain Cave, East Necluda.

items.name: Bokoblin Mask
items.effect: Disguise: Bokoblins won't attack unless provoked. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: From Koltin in exchange for Bubbul Gems.

items.name: Cap of the Hero
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of the Hero set (classic green-tunic homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Tuft Mountain area, East Necluda Depths.

items.name: Cap of the Sky
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of the Sky set (Skyward Sword homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Chest in Retsam Forest Cave (the Depths).

items.name: Cap of the Wild
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of the Wild set (BotW homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Chest in the Hebra Headspring Cave / Hebra region Depths.

items.name: Cap of the Wind
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of the Wind set (Wind Waker homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Chest accessed via the Tingel Island Chasm (the Depths).

items.name: Cap of Time
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of Time set (Ocarina of Time homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Sturnida Basin / Hebra Mountain Depths.

items.name: Cap of Twilight
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of Twilight set (Twilight Princess homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Gleeok Den, Great Hyrule Forest Depths (also via Twilight Princess / Smash amiibo).

items.name: Cece Hat
items.effect: No innate effect (a fashion hat). Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Reward for completing The Mayoral Election side adventure (Hateno Village).

items.name: Champion's Leathers
items.effect: Standalone single piece (no head/legs, no set bonus); lets Link fire Master Sword sword-beams even when not at full health.
items.where: Light the two fire pits in Hyrule Castle's Sanctum/throne room to open the tomb, then take it from inside.

items.name: Charged Headdress
items.effect: Stormy Weather Attack (attack synergy during thunderstorms). Part of the Charged set — set bonus 'Lightning Proof'.
items.where: Blocked ruin near Dracozu River (Faron region).

items.name: Charged Shirt
items.effect: Stormy Weather Attack (charge attacks faster in stormy weather); full set's 2-star bonus is Stormy Weather Charge.
items.where: Chest inside a ruin on the east side of Dracozu Lake (near the Spring of Courage).

items.name: Charged Trousers
items.effect: Stormy Weather Attack; the Charged set bonus 'Stormy Weather Charge' (faster charge attacks during storms) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest inside a blocked ruin in Damel Forest.

items.name: Climber's Bandanna
items.effect: Climbing Speed Up. Part of the Climbing Gear set — set bonus 'Climbing Jump Stamina Up'.
items.where: Chest in Ploymus Mountain Cave (Lanayru / Zora region).

items.name: Climbing Boots
items.effect: Climb Speed Up; the Climbing Gear set bonus 'Climbing Jump Stamina Up' (less stamina to jump while climbing) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest inside Upland Zorana Byroad (cave), south of Upland Zorana Skyview Tower.

items.name: Climbing Gear
items.effect: Climb Speed Up; full set's 2-star bonus is Climbing Jump Stamina Up (less stamina used when jumping while climbing).
items.where: Enter North Hyrule Plain Cave and go behind the waterfalls underneath.

items.name: Dark Hood
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Dark set — set bonus 'Night Speed Up'.
items.where: Bought from a Bargainer Statue in the Depths (for Poes).

items.name: Dark Trousers
items.effect: No special property; the Dark (Sheikah-look) set bonus 'Night Speed Up' unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Pray at the third Bargainer Statue in the Depths and buy for 200 Poes.

items.name: Dark Tunic
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Dark set's 2-star bonus is Night Speed Up (faster movement at night).
items.where: Trade 150 Poes at a Bargainer Statue in the Depths.

items.name: Desert Voe Headband
items.effect: Heat Resistance. Part of the Desert Voe set — set bonus 'Shock Damage Resist'.
items.where: Bought at the Gerudo clothing shop in Gerudo Town (or Kara Kara Bazaar).

items.name: Desert Voe Spaulder
items.effect: Heat Resistance; full set's 2-star bonus is Shock Damage Resist (reduces electric-attack damage).
items.where: Purchase from the Gerudo Secret Club in Gerudo Town.

items.name: Desert Voe Trousers
items.effect: Heat Resistance; the Desert Voe set bonus 'Shock Damage Resist' (reduces electric attack damage) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Buy from the Gerudo Secret Club shop in Gerudo Town.

items.name: Diamond Circlet
items.effect: Attack Up (raises attack power). Standalone jewelry — no set bonus; upgrades with Diamonds.
items.where: Bought at Starlight Memories jewelry shop, Gerudo Town (rupees + Diamond).

items.name: Ember Headdress
items.effect: Hot-Weather Attack (attack boost in hot climates). Part of the Ember set — set bonus 'Heat Emission'/related.
items.where: Chest in YunoboCo HQ South Cave (Eldin).

items.name: Ember Shirt
items.effect: Hot Weather Attack (charge attacks faster in hot weather); full Ember set's 2-star bonus is Hot Weather Charge.
items.where: Chest inside Goronbi River Cave.

items.name: Ember Trousers
items.effect: Hot Weather Attack (attack boost during hot weather); the Ember set bonus 'Hot Weather Attack' (greater hot-weather attack) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest inside Cephla Lake Cave in Eldin Canyon.

items.name: Evil Spirit Armor
items.effect: Stealth Up; full Evil Spirit set's 2-star bonus is Disguise + Bone Weapon Proficiency (Stal enemies ignore you and bone weapons hit harder).
items.where: Complete The Lomei Labyrinth Island prophecy side quest (the three labyrinths).

items.name: Evil Spirit Greaves
items.effect: Stealth Up; the Evil Spirit set bonus 'Disguise / Bone Weapon Proficiency' (Stal enemies ignore you, boosts bone-weapon damage) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Complete 'The North Lomei Prophecy' side quest.

items.name: Evil Spirit Mask
items.effect: Stealth Up. Part of the Evil Spirit set — full set grants Disguise (Stal-type) + Bone-weapon proficiency (innate, no upgrade needed).
items.where: Reward for the The South Lomei Labyrinth treasure (Gerudo Highlands / South Lomei Labyrinth).

items.name: Fierce Deity Armor
items.effect: Attack Up; full Fierce Deity set's 2-star bonus is Charge Atk. Stamina Up (less stamina used by charged attacks).
items.where: Chest inside Akkala Citadel Ruins Summit Cave.

items.name: Fierce Deity Boots
items.effect: Attack Up; the Fierce Deity set bonus 'Charge Atk. Stamina Up' (less stamina for charged attacks) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest at the Ancient Tree Stump (Misko's Treasure quest); also via the Majora's Mask / Fierce Deity amiibo.

items.name: Fierce Deity Mask
items.effect: Attack Up. Part of the Fierce Deity set — full set bonus 'Charge Atk. Stamina Up'. Obtainable in the world (not amiibo-locked).
items.where: Chest atop the rock in the back of Skull Lake Cave (north Akkala), after defeating the Stalnox.

items.name: Flamebreaker Armor
items.effect: Flame Guard (protects from environmental heat/flame); full set's 2-star bonus is Fireproof (full protection from fire).
items.where: Buy from the Armor Shop in Goron City (also stocked near Darunia Lake).

items.name: Flamebreaker Boots
items.effect: Flame Guard (resists burning environments); the Flamebreaker set bonus 'Fireproof' (cannot catch fire) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Buy at the Armor Shop in Goron City.

items.name: Flamebreaker Helm
items.effect: Flame Guard (protects from heat/flame in Eldin). Part of the Flamebreaker set — set bonus 'Fireproof'.
items.where: Bought at the Goron City Armor Shop, Eldin.

items.name: Froggy Hood
items.effect: Slip Resistance (climb in the rain / on ice without slipping). Part of the Froggy set — full set grants full Slip Resistance.
items.where: Reward for the Potential Princess Sightings! side adventure (complete 12 Penn stable quests); re-buyable at Cece's shop, Hateno.

items.name: Froggy Leggings
items.effect: Slip Resistance (better grip when climbing in rain); the Froggy set bonus 'Slip Proof' (no slipping while climbing in rain) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Reward for completing 9 of Penn's Side Adventures (the news-reporter quest chain) at different stables.

items.name: Froggy Sleeve
items.effect: Slip Resistance; full set's 2-star bonus is Slip Proof (prevents slipping when climbing in the rain).
items.where: Reward from the Potential Princess Sightings! side adventure at the Lucky Clover Gazette (complete the photo objectives).

items.name: Frostbite Headdress
items.effect: Cold-Weather Attack (attack boost in cold climates). Part of the Frostbite set — related cold set bonus.
items.where: Lake Kilsie Cave, Hebra Mountains.

items.name: Frostbite Shirt
items.effect: Cold Weather Attack (charge attacks faster in cold weather); full Frostbite set's 2-star bonus is Cold Weather Charge.
items.where: Chest inside Brightcap Cave.

items.name: Frostbite Trousers
items.effect: Cold Weather Attack (attack boost in cold weather); the Frostbite set bonus 'Cold Weather Attack' (greater cold-weather attack) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest inside Hebra Headspring Cave.

items.name: Gaiters of the Depths
items.effect: Gloom Resistance; the Depths set bonus 'Gloom Attack Resist' (adds an empty blue heart vs. Gloom-inflicting attacks) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Pray at the fourth Bargainer Statue in the Depths and buy for 200 Poes.

items.name: Glide Mask
items.effect: Skydive Mobility Up (faster control while diving). Part of the Glide set — set bonus 'Impact Proof' (no fall damage).
items.where: Reward for the dive challenge at Valor Island (Sky).

items.name: Glide Shirt
items.effect: Skydive Mobility Up; full set's 2-star bonus is Impact Proof (completely negates fall damage).
items.where: On Courage Island in the sky above Hyrule Ridge: give the Steward Construct a Zonai Charge and complete the Dive Ceremony (pass all rings in time).

items.name: Glide Tights
items.effect: Skydive Mobility Up; the Glide set bonus 'Impact Proof' (negates all fall damage) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Talk to the Steward Construct on Bravery Island and complete the Dive Ceremony.

items.name: Hood of the Depths
items.effect: Gloom Resistance. Part of the Depths set — set bonus 'Sticky Frog'/enhanced effect at full set.
items.where: Bought from a Bargainer Statue in the Depths (for Poes).

items.name: Horriblin Mask
items.effect: Disguise: Horriblins won't attack unless provoked. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: From Koltin in exchange for Bubbul Gems.

items.name: Hylian Hood
items.effect: No innate effect (basic shop gear). Part of the Hylian set (no set bonus).
items.where: Bought at general/clothing shops (e.g., Lookout Landing).

items.name: Hylian Trousers
items.effect: No special property; Hylian Set has no set bonus.
items.where: Buy at the Armor Shop in Lookout Landing.

items.name: Hylian Tunic
items.effect: No special effect and no set bonus; a basic, cheap defensive tunic.
items.where: Buy at the Armor Shop in Lookout Landing.

items.name: Island Lobster Shirt
items.effect: Heat Resistance; the Island Lobster Shirt is a single body piece with no set bonus.
items.where: Chest under the ship near Lurelin Village (use Ultrahand to reach it).

items.name: Korok Mask
items.effect: Shakes when a hidden Korok is nearby (aids Korok hunting). Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in the Forest Coliseum (the Depths) — appears after defeating the Black Hinox there.

items.name: Lightning Helm
items.effect: Lightning Proof (immune to lightning strikes; safe in storms with metal gear). Standalone (shares the Yiga aesthetic), no set bonus.
items.where: Reward for clearing all 3 Yiga Blademaster training challenges at the Yiga Clan Hideout, Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Lizalfos Mask
items.effect: Disguise: Lizalfos won't attack unless provoked. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: From Koltin in exchange for Bubbul Gems.

items.name: Lynel Mask
items.effect: Disguise: Lynels are fooled briefly but eventually see through it and attack. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: From Koltin in exchange for Bubbul Gems (the final mask).

items.name: Majora's Mask
items.effect: Makes it harder for most lesser enemies to spot you / they won't attack on sight. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in the Floating Coliseum (the Depths).

items.name: Mask of Awakening
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Of Awakening set (Link's Awakening homage) — set bonus 'Master Sword Beam Up'.
items.where: Thundra Plateau Cave (Hyrule Ridge).

items.name: Midna's Helmet
items.effect: Gloom Resistance (reduces gloom damage). Standalone, no set bonus; cannot be upgraded.
items.where: Gloom-locked chest in the Lone Island Coliseum (via the Eventide Island Chasm, Necluda Sea Depths) after clearing the Bokoblin waves.

items.name: Miner's Mask
items.effect: Glow (lights up dark areas like the Depths). Part of the Miner's set — set bonus 'Attack Up' (in the dark).
items.where: Abandoned Kara Kara Mine, Gerudo Desert Depths.

items.name: Miner's Top
items.effect: Glow (the piece glows in the dark); full set's 2-star bonus is Shining Steps (leaves a trail of glowing petals that light the area).
items.where: Chest in Daphnes Canyon Mine, Central Hyrule Depths.

items.name: Miner's Trousers
items.effect: Glow (the piece itself glows in the dark); the Miner's set bonus 'Shining Steps' (a glowing trail behind Link) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest in Hylia Canyon Mine, Central Hyrule Depths.

items.name: Moblin Mask
items.effect: Disguise: Moblins won't attack unless provoked. Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: From Koltin in exchange for Bubbul Gems.

items.name: Mystic Headpiece
items.effect: Rupee Padding (reduces money lost when defeated). Part of the Mystic set (no upgrade; from Koltin).
items.where: Exchanged from Koltin for Bubbul Gems.

items.name: Mystic Robe
items.effect: Rupee Padding (reduces rupees lost); Mystic set has no set bonus.
items.where: Received from Koltin in exchange for 3 Bubbul Gems during nighttime.

items.name: Mystic Trousers
items.effect: Rupee Padding (lose Rupees instead of health when hit); the Mystic Set has NO set bonus.
items.where: Purchase from Koltin (the wandering monster-fan) for 4 Bubbul Gems.

items.name: Opal Earrings
items.effect: Swim Speed Up. Standalone jewelry — no set bonus; upgrades with Opal.
items.where: Bought at Starlight Memories jewelry shop, Gerudo Town (rupees + Opal).

items.name: Phantom Armor
items.effect: Attack Up while equipped; the Phantom set has no set bonus, but each piece individually boosts attack.
items.where: Chest in Tamio River Downstream Cave, behind a breakable wall at the final section.

items.name: Phantom Greaves
items.effect: Attack Up; the Phantom set has no set bonus (high base defense, not upgradeable).
items.where: Chest inside the middle quicksand sinkhole in the Ancient Altar Ruins (Eastern Barrens, Gerudo Desert).

items.name: Phantom Helmet
items.effect: Attack Up. Part of the Phantom set (each piece grants Attack Up; the set has no separate set bonus).
items.where: Chest in Puffer Beach Overhead Cave (East Necluda) — entrance is on the cave ceiling, reach it with Zonai devices.

items.name: Radiant Mask
items.effect: Glows in the dark. Part of the Radiant set — full set, upgraded 2★, grants Disguise (Stal-type) + Bone-weapon proficiency.
items.where: Enchanted shop, Kakariko Village.

items.name: Radiant Shirt
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Radiant set's 2-star bonus is Disguise + Bone Weapon Proficiency (glows in the dark and Stal enemies ignore you).
items.where: Buy at Enchanted in Kakariko Village.

items.name: Radiant Tights
items.effect: No passive on this piece; the Radiant set bonus 'Disguise / Bone Weapon Proficiency' unlocks at 2 stars (Stal enemies ignore you).
items.where: Buy at Enchanted in Kakariko Village.

items.name: Ravio's Hood
items.effect: Sideways Climbing Speed Up (A Link Between Worlds homage). Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in the Secluded Coliseum (the Depths).

items.name: Royal Guard Boots
items.effect: No special property; Royal Guard Set has no set bonus.
items.where: Chest inside the King's Study in Hyrule Castle.

items.name: Royal Guard Cap
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Royal Guard set — set bonus 'Charge Atk. Stamina Up'.
items.where: Chest in Princess Zelda's Room, Hyrule Castle.

items.name: Royal Guard Uniform
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Royal Guard set's 2-star bonus is Charge Atk. Stamina Up (less stamina used by charged attacks).
items.where: Use Ascend on the cobwebbed ceiling within the Guards' Chamber (under Hyrule Castle).

items.name: Rubber Armor
items.effect: Shock Resistance; full set's 2-star bonus is Lightning Proof (immune to lightning strikes).
items.where: Chest inside Whistling Hill Cave.

items.name: Rubber Helm
items.effect: Shock Resistance. Part of the Rubber set — set bonus 'Lightning Proof'.
items.where: Chest in Sarjon Woods Cave (Hyrule Field / Floria River area).

items.name: Rubber Tights
items.effect: Shock/Lightning Resistance; the Rubber set bonus 'Lightning Proof' (immunity to lightning strikes) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest inside Horon Lagoon Cave.

items.name: Ruby Circlet
items.effect: Cold Resistance (heat retention in cold areas). Standalone jewelry — no set bonus; upgrades with Rubies.
items.where: Bought at Starlight Memories jewelry shop, Gerudo Town (rupees + Ruby).

items.name: Sand Boots
items.effect: Sand Speed Up (move normally on desert sand); standalone Gerudo-Secret-Club item with no set bonus.
items.where: Buy from the Gerudo Secret Club shop in Gerudo Town.

items.name: Sapphire Circlet
items.effect: Heat Resistance (cooling in hot areas). Standalone jewelry — no set bonus; upgrades with Sapphires.
items.where: Bought at Starlight Memories jewelry shop, Gerudo Town (rupees + Sapphire).

items.name: Sheik's Mask
items.effect: Stealth Up (Sheik homage). Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in the Desert Coliseum (the Depths).

items.name: Snow Boots
items.effect: Snow Speed Up (move normally through snow); standalone Gerudo-Secret-Club item with no set bonus.
items.where: Buy from the Gerudo Secret Club shop in Gerudo Town.

items.name: Snowquill Headdress
items.effect: Cold Resistance. Part of the Snowquill set — set bonus 'Unfreezable'.
items.where: Bought at the Brazen Beak clothing shop, Rito Village.

items.name: Snowquill Trousers
items.effect: Cold Resistance; the Snowquill set bonus 'Unfreezable' (prevents being frozen by ice attacks) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Buy at Brazen Beak in Rito Village.

items.name: Snowquill Tunic
items.effect: Cold Resistance; full set's 2-star bonus is Unfreezable (cannot be frozen by ice attacks).
items.where: Buy at Brazen Beak in Rito Village.

items.name: Soldier's Armor
items.effect: No effect and no set bonus; the Soldier set has the joint-highest base defense of any upgradeable ordinary set.
items.where: Chest inside the Royal Hidden Passage's jail cell (accessible after clearing a Temple during Regional Phenomena).

items.name: Soldier's Greaves
items.effect: No special property; Soldier's Set has no set bonus (highest base defense of an ordinary set).
items.where: Chest inside the Royal Hidden Passage (Hyrule Castle area).

items.name: Soldier's Helm
items.effect: No special effect. Part of the Soldier set (no set bonus).
items.where: Chest in the Royal Hidden Passage beneath Hyrule Castle.

items.name: Stealth Chest Guard
items.effect: Stealth Up (quieter movement); full Stealth set's 2-star bonus is Night Speed Up.
items.where: Buy at Enchanted in Kakariko Village.

items.name: Stealth Mask
items.effect: Stealth Up (quieter movement). Part of the Stealth set — set bonus 'Night Speed Up'.
items.where: Enchanted shop, Kakariko Village.

items.name: Stealth Tights
items.effect: Stealth Up; the Stealth (Sheikah) set bonus 'Night Speed Up' unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Buy at Enchanted in Kakariko Village.

items.name: Tingle's Hood
items.effect: No innate effect. Part of the Tingle set — set bonus 'Night Speed Up'.
items.where: Light-puzzle reward at the Statue of the Eighth Heroine (Gerudo Highlands).

items.name: Tingle's Shirt
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Tingle set's 2-star bonus is Night Speed Up.
items.where: Chest in Dueling Peaks South Cave.

items.name: Tingle's Tights
items.effect: No special property; the Tingle set bonus 'Night Speed Up' unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest in Cape Cales Cliffbase Cave.

items.name: Topaz Earrings
items.effect: Shock Resistance (reduces electric damage). Standalone jewelry — no set bonus; upgrades with Topaz.
items.where: Bought at Starlight Memories jewelry shop, Gerudo Town (rupees + Topaz).

items.name: Trousers of Awakening
items.effect: No special property; standalone legacy piece, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in Coliseum Ruins Cave, Hyrule Field; also via amiibo.

items.name: Trousers of the Hero
items.effect: No special property; standalone legacy piece, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in Corvash Canyon Mine, Hebra Mountains Depths; also via the Link amiibo.

items.name: Trousers of the Sky
items.effect: No special property; standalone legacy piece, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest at Crenel Canyon Mine (Depths); also via amiibo.

items.name: Trousers of the Wild
items.effect: No special property; the Of the Wild set bonus 'Attack Up' unlocks at 2 stars (full set required).
items.where: Chest in the Eldin Canyon Dark Skeleton (Depths).
items.set: Tunic of the Wild (Of the Wild Set)

items.name: Trousers of the Wind
items.effect: No special property; standalone legacy piece, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest at Cresia Pit Mine, East Necluda Depths; also via amiibo.

items.name: Trousers of Time
items.effect: No special property; standalone amiibo / mine-chest legacy piece, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in Gerudo Canyon Mine (Depths); also via the Skyward Sword / Ocarina-of-Time amiibo.

items.name: Trousers of Twilight
items.effect: No special property; standalone legacy piece, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in Ancient Underground Fortress, Akkala Highlands Depths; also via the Smash Bros / Twilight Princess Link amiibo.

items.name: Tunic of Awakening
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Hero of Awakening set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest at Ancient Columns Cave, Tabantha Frontier.

items.name: Tunic of Memories
items.effect: Standalone tunic with no effect and no set bonus (cosmetic memento).
items.where: Find all the Bargainer Statues in the Depths and trade 400 Poes (the A Call from the Depths / Bargainer reward).

items.name: Tunic of the Depths
items.effect: Gloom Resistance; full Depths set's 2-star bonus is Gloom Attack Resist (one extra heart of gloom protection).
items.where: Buy for 150 Poes from the second Bargainer Statue (in the Depths) after praying at the first.

items.name: Tunic of the Hero
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Hero set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest in Dueling Canyons Mine, West Necluda Depths.

items.name: Tunic of the Sky
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Hero of the Sky set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest at Minshi Grove, Great Hyrule Forest Depths.

items.name: Tunic of the Wild
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Of the Wild set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest in Gerudo Dark Skeleton (Gerudo Desert Depths), near the skeleton's jaw.
items.set: Tunic of the Wild (Of the Wild Set)

items.name: Tunic of the Wind
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Hero of Winds set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest at Cuho Canyon Mine, Tabantha Frontier Depths.

items.name: Tunic of Time
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Hero of Time set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest in Lindor Canyon Mine, Hyrule Ridge Depths.

items.name: Tunic of Twilight
items.effect: No standalone effect; full Hero of Twilight set's 2-star bonus is Attack Up.
items.where: Chest in Rist Mine, Akkala Sea Depths.

items.name: Vah Medoh Divine Helm
items.effect: Cold Resistance. Part of the Divine Beast set, no set bonus.
items.where: North Biron Snowshelf Cave (Hebra); also via Revali amiibo.

items.name: Vah Naboris Divine Helm
items.effect: Shock Resistance. Part of the Divine Beast set, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in the West Gerudo Ruins (Treasure of the Gerudo Desert side quest); also via Urbosa amiibo.

items.name: Vah Rudania Divine Helm
items.effect: Flame Guard. Part of the Divine Beast set, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in a cave near Death Mountain, Eldin Canyon (also via Daruk amiibo).

items.name: Vah Ruta Divine Helm
items.effect: Swim Speed Up. Part of the Divine Beast set, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in a cave behind a waterfall at Zora's Domain (also via Mipha amiibo).

items.name: Well-Worn Hair Band
items.effect: No effect (starter/cosmetic hair band). Part of the starter/Archaic look (no set bonus).
items.where: Chest down the well in Hateno Village.

items.name: Yiga Armor
items.effect: Stealth Up; full Yiga set's 2-star bonus is Night Speed Up.
items.where: Defeat the Yiga Clan in the former Akkala Ancient Tech Lab, then talk to Konba.

items.name: Yiga Mask
items.effect: Stealth Up (low defense). Part of the Yiga set — set bonus 'Night Speed Up'.
items.where: Great Plateau shack — drops after defeating the Yiga Footsoldier there.

items.name: Yiga Tights
items.effect: Stealth Up; the Yiga set bonus 'Charge Attack Stamina Up' unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Defeat the Yiga Clan, then use Ascend and talk to Aliza at Maritta Branch (Karusa Valley hideout).

items.name: Zant's Helmet
items.effect: Unfreezable (cannot be frozen by ice attacks). Standalone, no set bonus.
items.where: Chest in the Scorching Coliseum (Eldin Canyon Depths), after clearing all the Gloom Moblin waves.

items.name: Zonaite Helm
items.effect: Energy Up. Part of the Zonaite Set — set bonus 'Energy Recharge Up'.
items.where: Mirror/light-puzzle reward at Lightcast Island (Tabantha Frontier Sky).

items.name: Zonaite Shin Guards
items.effect: Energy Up (slows battery drain); the Zonaite set bonus 'Energy Recharge Up' (faster battery recharge) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Chest inside a floating sky sphere/globe — use Ultrahand to place the giant block on the platform.

items.name: Zonaite Waistguard
items.effect: Energy Up (improves battery use); full set's 2-star bonus is Energy Recharge Up (doubles battery recharge speed).
items.where: Chest on Zonaite Forge Island, East Necluda Sky.

items.name: Zora Armor
items.effect: Swim Speed Up; full set's 2-star bonus is Swim Dash Stamina Up (less stamina used when swim-dashing).
items.where: Receive from the Zora storyline (Mipha's gift, repaired during the Zora questline).

items.name: Zora Greaves
items.effect: Swim Speed Up; the Zora set bonus 'Swim Dash Stamina Up' (less stamina when swim-dashing) unlocks at 2 stars.
items.where: Complete the 'A Token of Friendship' side quest after clearing the Water Temple.

items.name: Zora Helm
items.effect: Swim Speed Up. Part of the Zora set — set bonus 'Swim Dash Stamina Up'.
items.where: Cave on Floating Scales Island (Lanayru / Zora region), reward for The Never-Ending Lecture side quest.

items.name: Aerocuda Eyeball
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +4 attack and homing accuracy (auto-aims).
items.where: Drops from Aerocuda (flying eel-like enemies) across Hyrule.

items.name: Aerocuda Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +4 attack and extra projectile distance.
items.where: Drops from Aerocuda across Hyrule and the Depths.

items.name: Amber
items.effect: Fuse for a solid +4 attack (no element) — the cheapest strong early melee fuse. Also one of the most-used armor-upgrade materials, demanded by Great Fairies across many sets.
items.where: Break ore deposits in caves, mountains, and mines; drops from Battle Talus. Sell in bulk to Ramella in Goron City when she's buying it.

items.name: Black Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +17 attack; solid budget weapon/arrow fuse.
items.where: Drops from Black Bokoblins (tougher camps; common in the Depths).

items.name: Black Boss Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +27 attack; strong two-handed/weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Black Boss Bokoblins (tough camp leaders, common in the Depths).

items.name: Black Hinox Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +27 attack; powerful heavy-weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Black Hinox (toughest Hinox; the Depths).

items.name: Black Horriblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +22 attack; strong late-game fuse.
items.where: Drops from Black Horriblins in caves and the Depths.

items.name: Black Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +26 attack; strong late-game fuse.
items.where: Drops from Black Lizalfos (tough areas; common in the Depths).

items.name: Black Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +24 attack plus extended reach (whip-like attacks).
items.where: Drops from Black Lizalfos (tougher areas; common in the Depths).

items.name: Black Moblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +24 attack; strong heavy-weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Black Moblins (tougher camps; common in the Depths).

items.name: Blue Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +7 attack; cheap mid-tier arrow/weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue Bokoblins (mid-tier camps across Hyrule and the Depths).

items.name: Blue Boss Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack; reliable mid-tier weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue Boss Bokoblins (Hyrule Field, Lanayru Great Spring, the Depths).

items.name: Blue Hinox Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +19 attack; strong heavy-weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue Hinox (tougher areas).

items.name: Blue Horriblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +11 attack; mid-tier fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue Horriblins in caves and Depths chasms.

items.name: Blue Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack; good mid-tier fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue Lizalfos (mid-tier areas and the Depths).

items.name: Blue Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack with extra reach.
items.where: Drops from Blue Lizalfos (mid-tier areas).

items.name: Blue Moblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +13 attack; good mid-tier weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue Moblins (mid-tier camps).

items.name: Blue-Maned Lynel Mace Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +29 attack; elite two-handed weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue-Maned Lynels.

items.name: Blue-Maned Lynel Saber Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +33 attack; elite one-handed weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Blue-Maned Lynels (Lanayru Great Spring, Eldin Depths).

items.name: Blue-White Frox Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +35 attack; tears through bedrock and ore.
items.where: Drops from Blue-White Frox in the Depths.

items.name: Bokoblin Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +2 attack; also used to upgrade early armor sets.
items.where: Drops from Bokoblins of all colors and from Stalkoblins.

items.name: Bokoblin Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1 (blunt); mainly an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Blue Bokoblins or higher (not from common red ones).

items.name: Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon or arrow for +4 attack; also an early armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from common red Bokoblins all over Hyrule's surface and the Depths.

items.name: Bomb Flower
items.effect: Throw it as a grenade, or fuse to an arrow (+1 attack) to make a Bomb Arrow, or to a weapon/shield, for an explosion that damages groups, breaks rocks, and knocks enemies back.
items.where: Grows in the Depths (very common around the Great Plateau / Hyrule Field underground) and in caves.

items.name: Boss Bokoblin Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +6 attack; modest weapon/arrow fuse.
items.where: Drops from Boss Bokoblins of all colors.

items.name: Boss Bokoblin Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +6; mainly an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Boss Bokoblins of all colors.

items.name: Boss Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +10 attack; good sturdy weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from red Boss Bokoblins (camp leaders) on the surface and in the Depths.

items.name: Brightbloom Seed
items.effect: Throw or strike it to make it bloom into a small light source — the core tool for lighting up the pitch-black Depths and caves. Fused to an arrow it lights wherever it lands (+1 attack); on a shield it lights on a hit.
items.where: Harvested from cave walls and all over the Depths.

items.name: Captain Construct I Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +5 attack; basic Captain-Construct fuse.
items.where: Drops from Captain Construct I in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Captain Construct II Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +15 attack; good mid-tier construct fuse.
items.where: Drops from Captain Construct II in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Captain Construct III Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +25 attack; strong construct fuse.
items.where: Drops from Captain Construct III in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Captain Construct IV Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +35 attack; the best Construct weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Captain Construct IV in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Chuchu Jelly
items.effect: Fuse for +1; throw or fuse for splash damage, and an armor-upgrade/elixir material.
items.where: Drops from common Chuchus everywhere.

items.name: Crystallized Charge
items.effect: A battery-upgrade currency: take 100 to a Crystal Refinery's Steward Construct for one Energy Well, which permanently expands your Energy Cell. Made by refining Zonaite at green Forge Constructs (3 Zonaite -> 1 charge). Not a fuse/cook material — it exists to grow your battery.
items.where: Refine Zonaite ore at the green Forge Constructs at mining outposts across the Depths; 3 Large Zonaite refines into 1 Large Crystallized Charge (worth 20 ordinary charges).

items.name: Dazzlefruit
items.effect: Throw it, or fuse it to a weapon/arrow (+1 attack), to detonate a flash of dazzling light that blinds and stuns nearby monsters. Useful against groups and Keese/Like Likes.
items.where: Grows in the Depths and some surface caves, often near other glowing flora.

items.name: Diamond
items.effect: The strongest gem fuse: a flat +25 attack with no element — turns a plain weapon into a heavy hitter. Also used to upgrade the Diamond Circlet.
items.where: Break rare (golden) ore deposits in caves/mountains; drops from Stone Talus (Rare) and the Igneo/Frost Talus; buyable (3 at a time) at the Goron City ore stall for 1,000 rupees each; sell 10 to Ramella for 5,500.

items.name: Dinraal's Claw
items.effect: Fuse for +18 attack and a fire effect on weapons/arrows/shields. A rarer Dinraal upgrade material. Generally kept for upgrades, not sold.
items.where: Shoot Dinraal's feet/claws as the fire dragon circles the Eldin/Akkala skies (north Hyrule). Respawns on the blood moon; one part per ~10 minutes aloft.

items.name: Dinraal's Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +26 attack plus a fire effect (the strongest fire-dragon fuse) — on arrows it ignites, on shields it adds fire on parry. The premier fire-themed upgrade material.
items.where: Shoot the two large horns on Dinraal's head — the rarest body part. Dinraal flies the northern (Eldin/Akkala) sky; aim for the horns specifically.

items.name: Dinraal's Scale
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon/arrow/shield for +16 attack and a fire effect. Drops from Dinraal's body; a fire-themed armor-upgrade material. Best saved, not sold.
items.where: Shoot or touch Dinraal's body (do not try to kill it — dragons are immortal). Dinraal, the fire dragon, flies the northern Eldin sky, emerging from a chasm in Akkala north of Tarrey Town and circling Death Mountain. One part drops per ~10 real minutes.

items.name: Electric Keese Eyeball
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +6 attack, homing, and a shock effect.
items.where: Drops from Electric Keese in the Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Electric Keese Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +2 attack, more range, and a shock effect.
items.where: Drops from Electric Keese in the Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Electric Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +15 attack with a shock effect (strips metal gear from foes).
items.where: Drops from Electric Lizalfos in the Gerudo Desert and Faron.

items.name: Electric Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +10 attack and reach with a shock effect.
items.where: Drops from Electric Lizalfos in the Gerudo Desert and Faron.

items.name: Farosh's Claw
items.effect: Fuse for +18 attack and a shock effect on weapons/arrows/shields. A rarer Farosh upgrade material. Usually kept.
items.where: Shoot Farosh's feet/claws as the lightning dragon circles Lake Hylia / the Gerudo region. One part per ~10 minutes.

items.name: Farosh's Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +26 attack and a shock effect (Farosh's strongest fuse) — electrifies on hit, adds shock to arrows and shield parries. The top shock-themed upgrade material.
items.where: Shoot Farosh's horns — its rarest part. Farosh ranges Lake Hylia, Faron and the Gerudo region.

items.name: Farosh's Scale
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon/arrow/shield for +16 attack and a shock/electric effect. Farosh's body material; a shock-themed armor-upgrade material. Best kept.
items.where: Touch or shoot Farosh's body. Farosh, the lightning dragon, rises from the East Gerudo region and flies across Lake Hylia into Faron, returning to the Depths via the Hills of Baumer chasm (roughly a 15-minute loop, active around dawn).

items.name: Fire Keese Eyeball
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +8 attack, homing, and a fire effect.
items.where: Drops from Fire Keese in Eldin and hot regions.

items.name: Fire Keese Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +2 attack, more range, and a fire effect.
items.where: Drops from Fire Keese in Eldin and hot regions.

items.name: Fire Like Stone
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack with a fire effect.
items.where: Coughed up by Fire Like Likes in the Eldin Mountains and Depths.

items.name: Fire-Breath Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +15 attack with a fire effect (sets things alight / melts ice).
items.where: Drops from Fire-Breath Lizalfos in Eldin Canyon and Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Fire-Breath Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +10 attack and reach with a fire effect.
items.where: Drops from Fire-Breath Lizalfos in Eldin Canyon and Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Flint
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon, shield, or arrow for a small +1 attack (no special property). Its real use is utility: drop Flint on firewood and strike it with a metal weapon to spark a campfire (the Flint breaks after one use). Also a cooking ingredient.
items.where: Break ore deposits in Hyrule's caves, mountains, mines, and wells; also drops from rock-type enemies. Very common.

items.name: Frox Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +14 attack; can smash ore deposits and rocks.
items.where: Drops from Frox in the Depths.

items.name: Frox Fingernail
items.effect: Fuse for +10 attack; also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Frox of all types in the Depths.

items.name: Frox Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1; mainly an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Frox in the Depths.

items.name: Giant Brightbloom Seed
items.effect: A far brighter Brightbloom Seed — throw or strike it and it roots into a large flower radiating a wide pool of light (lights big sections of the Depths). Fuses to arrows/shields for light (+1 attack).
items.where: Found deeper in the Depths, often near Lightroots and large caverns; much rarer than the regular seed.

items.name: Gibdo Bone
items.effect: Fuse for +40 attack — a strong, cheap-to-stockpile weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Gibdos in the Gerudo Desert (Gloom era) and the Depths.

items.name: Gibdo Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1; mainly an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Gibdos in the Gerudo region.

items.name: Gibdo Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +8 attack and extra projectile distance.
items.where: Drops from flying/winged Gibdos in the Gerudo region.

items.name: Gleeok Flame Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +30 attack with a powerful fire effect.
items.where: Drops from Fire Gleeoks on the surface and in the Depths.

items.name: Gleeok Frost Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +30 attack with a powerful freezing effect.
items.where: Drops from Frost Gleeoks on the surface and in the Depths.

items.name: Gleeok Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1; best kept — a top-tier Attack-Up elixir ingredient and a high-value sell.
items.where: Drops rarely from Gleeoks of all elements.

items.name: Gleeok Thunder Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +30 attack with a powerful shock effect.
items.where: Drops from Thunder Gleeoks on the surface and in the Depths.

items.name: Gleeok Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow/weapon for +24 attack and great projectile reach.
items.where: Drops from Gleeoks of all elements.

items.name: Hinox Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1; valued as an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Hinox of all colors.

items.name: Hinox Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack; sturdy heavy-weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from red Hinox sleeping across Hyrule's fields and forests.

items.name: Hinox Toenail
items.effect: Fuse for +7 attack; tough shield/weapon fuse and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Hinox of all colors.

items.name: Hinox Tooth
items.effect: Fuse for +8 attack; also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Hinox of all colors.

items.name: Horriblin Claw
items.effect: Fuse for +4 attack; modest weapon fuse, also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Horriblins of all colors.

items.name: Horriblin Guts
items.effect: Fuse for a small bonus; mainly an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Blue Horriblins or higher.

items.name: Horriblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +5 attack; modest weapon/arrow fuse.
items.where: Drops from Horriblins clinging to cave and Depths ceilings.

items.name: Ice Keese Eyeball
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +4 attack, homing, and a freezing effect.
items.where: Drops from Ice Keese in Hebra and Mount Lanayru.

items.name: Ice Keese Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +2 attack, more range, and a freezing effect.
items.where: Drops from Ice Keese in cold regions.

items.name: Ice Like Stone
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack with a freezing effect.
items.where: Coughed up by Ice Like Likes on Mount Lanayru and the Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Ice-Breath Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +15 attack with a freezing effect.
items.where: Drops from Ice-Breath Lizalfos in Hebra Mountains and Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Ice-Breath Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +10 attack and reach with a freezing effect.
items.where: Drops from Ice-Breath Lizalfos in Hebra Mountains and Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Keese Eyeball
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for homing accuracy (auto-aims at the nearest foe); no attack bonus.
items.where: Drops from common Keese (caves, ruins, night skies).

items.name: Keese Wing
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for extra projectile distance; no attack bonus.
items.where: Drops from common Keese.

items.name: King's Scale
items.effect: Fuse for +5 attack; a parry/guard-up boon, but it shatters after one fused hit. Cannot be sold.
items.where: Given in sets of 5 by King Dorephan in Zora's Domain (Sidon's questline / after the Water Temple).

items.name: Korok Frond
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon (+1 attack) to make a 'Korok Frond Guster' — a fan that creates a burst of wind when swung, used to push sail-boats/objects, clear gas/fog, and fan flames.
items.where: Found around Korok Forest and on some sky platforms; sometimes near Koroks. A fairly uncommon material.

items.name: Large Zonai Charge
items.effect: A bigger Zonai Charge — consume it to refill a larger chunk of the Energy Cell battery at once. Can also be fused/cooked like the regular charge.
items.where: Dropped by tougher Zonai constructs (especially in the Depths) and from capsule dispensers; rarer than the standard Zonai Charge.

items.name: Large Zonaite
items.effect: A richer chunk of Zonaite that converts into far more Crystallized Charges at a Forge Construct, speeding up battery/Energy Cell and Autobuild upgrades. Fuse gives only +1 attack.
