items.where: Rarer Depths drop: from Zonaite deposits and large wall deposits, and dropped by Frox variants (best from the stronger Frox); one is a reward from Josha's 'A Mystery in the Depths' quest line.

items.name: Light Dragon's Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +20 attack with the heal-on-hit effect — the best sustain fuse in the game (no element, restores health on every hit). The Light Dragon's rarest part.
items.where: Shoot the Light Dragon's horns during its flight over Hyrule. Rarest of its drops; one part per ~10 minutes.

items.name: Light Dragon's Scale
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack with a healing effect — restores a bit of health each time the fused weapon lands a hit. From the Light Dragon's body; a unique self-heal fuse and upgrade material.
items.where: Touch or shoot the Light Dragon's body. The Light Dragon circles the surface of all Hyrule and never enters the Depths; it flies very high until you cure the Great Deku Tree, after which it descends to sky-island altitude. Available throughout the game.

items.name: Light Dragon's Talon
items.effect: Fuse for +14 attack with the same heal-on-hit effect. The Light Dragon's foot/claw material.
items.where: Shoot the Light Dragon's feet/talons during its flight around Hyrule's surface.

items.name: Like Like Stone
items.effect: Fuse for +4 attack; a heavy blunt fuse and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Coughed up by Like Likes in Hyrule Field and the Eldin Mountains.

items.name: Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +8 attack; decent light-weapon/arrow fuse.
items.where: Drops from common Lizalfos (Lanayru Wetlands, water and forest areas).

items.name: Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +6 attack and extends a weapon's reach (whip-like attacks); good on spears.
items.where: Drops from common Lizalfos (Lanayru Wetlands and water areas).

items.name: Lizalfos Talon
items.effect: Fuse for +5 attack; modest weapon fuse, also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Lizalfos of various colors.

items.name: Luminous Stone
items.effect: Glowing stone. Fuse gives only +1 attack with NO special property (a weak fuse). Its real uses: required to upgrade the Radiant and Awakening armor sets, and you can drop up to 5 near a Dondon, wait, and get a random ore back.
items.where: Break glowing (luminous) ore deposits in dark caves and around mountains.

items.name: Lynel Guts
items.effect: Best kept for cooking — makes a tier-3 Attack-Up elixir and sells high; only a +1 fuse so don't fuse it.
items.where: Drops rarely from Lynels of all manes.

items.name: Lynel Hoof
items.effect: Fuse for +10 attack; also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Lynels of all manes.

items.name: Lynel Mace Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +18 attack; excellent on two-handed weapons.
items.where: Drops from common (maneless) Lynels.

items.name: Lynel Saber Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +22 attack; one of the best one-handed weapon fuses in the game.
items.where: Drops from common (maneless) Lynels on the surface and in the Depths.

items.name: Moblin Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +4 attack; modest weapon/arrow fuse.
items.where: Drops from Moblins of all colors and from Stalmoblins.

items.name: Moblin Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1 (blunt); valued as an Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Blue Moblins or higher.

items.name: Moblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +6 attack; reliable two-handed weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from red Moblins across Hyrule and the Depths.

items.name: Molduga Fin
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack with added reach; also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Molduga in the Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Molduga Guts
items.effect: Fuse for +1; valued as a strong Attack-Up elixir ingredient and armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Molduga in the Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Molduga Jaw
items.effect: Fuse for +32 attack; a powerful heavy-weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Molduga, the sand sharks of the Gerudo Desert.

items.name: Muddle Bud
items.effect: Throw it, or fuse it to a weapon/arrow (+1 attack), to release a purple confusion cloud that muddles enemies into attacking each other for a short time — strong crowd control.
items.where: Grows in the Depths (often near the large trees underground).

items.name: Naydra's Claw
items.effect: Fuse for +18 attack and an ice effect on weapons/arrows/shields. A rarer Naydra upgrade material. Usually kept for upgrades.
items.where: Shoot Naydra's feet/claws as the ice dragon circles Mount Lanayru / East Necluda. One part per ~10 minutes.

items.name: Naydra's Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +26 attack and an ice effect (Naydra's strongest fuse) — chills/freezes on hit, adds ice to arrows and shield parries. The top ice-themed upgrade material.
items.where: Shoot Naydra's horns — its rarest part. Naydra circles Mount Lanayru and East Necluda.

items.name: Naydra's Scale
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon/arrow/shield for +16 attack and an ice/cold effect. Naydra's body material; an ice-themed armor-upgrade material. Best kept.
items.where: Touch or shoot Naydra's body. Naydra, the ice dragon, emerges from the Lanayru Snowfield chasm in front of Mount Lanayru (around 1 PM), circles the Hateno/East Necluda area, then dives back into the ground near East Hill above Kakariko.

items.name: Obsidian Frox Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +24 attack with strong ore/rock-crushing power.
items.where: Drops from Obsidian Frox in the Depths.

items.name: Octo Balloon
items.effect: Fuse to objects to make them float — a key Ultrahand building material.
items.where: Drops from Water Octoroks; also found on signboards and balloons.

items.name: Octorok Eyeball
items.effect: Fuse to an arrow for +3 attack and homing accuracy (auto-aims).
items.where: Drops from Octoroks of all types in water, forest, rock, and snow.

items.name: Octorok Tentacle
items.effect: Fuse for +3 attack; also an armor-upgrade material.
items.where: Drops from Octoroks of all types.

items.name: Opal
items.effect: Fuse Attack Power 1. On a weapon it shoots a water orb; on an arrow it adds AOE water damage that douses enemies and snuffs fire; on a shield a blocked attack douses attackers in water. Used to upgrade the Zora set (60 opals for the full ★4). One of the more common gems.
items.where: Break ore deposits in caves and mountains; small chance from melting snowballs with fire; found in some shrine chests. Common.

items.name: Puffshroom
items.effect: Throw it, or fuse it to a weapon/arrow (+1 attack), to burst a thick smoke cloud that hides Link — slip away or land sneakstrikes on the enemies inside it.
items.where: Grows in caves and the Depths, often near the large trees underground.

items.name: Red Chuchu Jelly
items.effect: Fuse for +1 with a fire effect (ignites grass, melts ice); throw it as a fire bomb.
items.where: Drops from Fire Chuchus in warm areas.

items.name: Rock Salt
items.effect: Mainly a cooking ingredient (salt-grilled meat/fish/veg, soups). Can also be fused for a minor +1 attack, but has no special fuse property.
items.where: Break ore deposits in caves and around mountains/mines — especially deposits near water/shores, which favor Rock Salt.

items.name: Ruby
items.effect: Fuse Attack Power 1. On a weapon it shoots a fireball; on an arrow it adds a large AOE fire explosion (the biggest fire fuse); on a shield it grants cold resistance and a small explosion when an attack is blocked. Used in armor upgrades (e.g. Ruby Circlet, Vah Rudania Divine Helm).
items.where: Break rare (golden) ore deposits in caves/mountains, especially Eldin/Death Mountain; common drop from Stone Talus (Rare) and Igneo Talus; silver-tier enemies can drop one.

items.name: Sage's Will
items.effect: A Sage-upgrade material: pray at a Goddess Statue and trade 4 Sage's Wills to upgrade one Sage's Vow to a Solemn Vow, boosting that Sage's avatar's attack power. A key item — it cannot be sold or fused.
items.where: Found in hidden/guarded treasure chests across the Sky archipelagos (Akkala, Central, Eldin, Faron, Gerudo, Hebra, Lanayru, Necluda skies); 20 total, enough to upgrade all 5 Sages.

items.name: Sapphire
items.effect: Fuse Attack Power 1. On a weapon it shoots an ice orb; on an arrow it adds AOE ice that freezes; on a shield it grants fire resistance and a freezing blast when an attack is blocked. Used in armor upgrades (e.g. Sapphire Circlet, Desert Voe set).
items.where: Break rare (golden) ore deposits in caves/mountains; drops from Frost Pebblit, Frost Talus, and Stone Talus (Rare); silver-tier enemies can drop one.

items.name: Shard of Dinraal's Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +20 attack and a fire effect. A high-value armor-upgrade material taken from Dinraal's mouth. Usually kept rather than sold.
items.where: Shoot the head/mouth of Dinraal (fire dragon) over Eldin/north Hyrule. Rarer drop; comes off the face area.

items.name: Shard of Dinraal's Spike
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack and a fire effect. The common spike shard mined from Dinraal's back; also used in cooking and lower-tier upgrades.
items.where: The glowing spikes along Dinraal's back detach when struck as it flies over Eldin; shoot or grab them.

items.name: Shard of Farosh's Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +20 attack and a shock effect. A high-value shock armor-upgrade material from Farosh's mouth.
items.where: Shoot the head/mouth of Farosh (lightning dragon) over Lake Hylia / the Gerudo Highlands.

items.name: Shard of Farosh's Spike
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack and a shock effect. The common spike shard from Farosh's back; also used for cooking and lower-tier upgrades.
items.where: Grab or shoot the glowing spikes along Farosh's back near Lake Hylia / Gerudo.

items.name: Shard of Light Dragon's Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack with the heal-on-hit effect. From the Light Dragon's mouth — strong sustain on a melee weapon.
items.where: Shoot the Light Dragon's head/mouth as it flies around Hyrule.

items.name: Shard of Light Dragon's Spike
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack with the heal-on-hit effect. The common spike shard from the Light Dragon's back.
items.where: Grab or shoot the glowing spikes along the Light Dragon's back during its flight.

items.name: Shard of Naydra's Fang
items.effect: Fuse for +20 attack and an ice effect. A high-value ice armor-upgrade material from Naydra's mouth.
items.where: Shoot the head/mouth of Naydra (ice dragon) over Lanayru / East Necluda.

items.name: Shard of Naydra's Spike
items.effect: Fuse for +16 attack and an ice effect. The common spike shard from Naydra's back; also used for cooking and lower-tier upgrades.
items.where: Grab or shoot the glowing spikes along Naydra's back as it flies near Mount Lanayru.

items.name: Shock Like Stone
items.effect: Fuse for +12 attack with a shock effect.
items.where: Coughed up by Shock Like Likes in Hyrule Field and East Necluda.

items.name: Silver Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +31 attack; strong late-game fuse from the toughest Bokoblins.
items.where: Drops from Silver Bokoblins (late-game; common in the Depths).

items.name: Silver Boss Bokoblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +37 attack; one of the better non-Lynel horn fuses.
items.where: Drops from Silver Boss Bokoblins (late-game leaders in the Depths).

items.name: Silver Horriblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +32 attack; top-tier Horriblin fuse.
items.where: Drops from Silver Horriblins (late-game caves and the Depths).

items.name: Silver Lizalfos Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +34 attack; one of the best non-Lynel horn fuses.
items.where: Drops from Silver Lizalfos (late-game; common in the Depths).

items.name: Silver Lizalfos Tail
items.effect: Fuse for +31 attack with extended reach; top-tier spear fuse.
items.where: Drops from Silver Lizalfos (late-game; common in the Depths).

items.name: Silver Lynel Mace Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +51 attack — the strongest two-handed weapon fuse in the game.
items.where: Drops from Silver Lynels (late-game; coliseum and the Depths).

items.name: Silver Lynel Saber Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +55 attack — the single strongest one-handed weapon fuse in the game.
items.where: Drops from Silver Lynels (late-game; coliseum and the Depths).

items.name: Silver Moblin Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +33 attack; top-tier non-Lynel weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Silver Moblins (late-game; common in the Depths).

items.name: Soldier Construct Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +3 attack; basic Zonai-construct weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from tier-I Soldier Constructs in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Soldier Construct II Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +8 attack; mid-tier construct fuse.
items.where: Drops from Soldier Construct II in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Soldier Construct III Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +18 attack; strong construct fuse.
items.where: Drops from Soldier Construct III in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Soldier Construct IV Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +24 attack; top Soldier-Construct fuse.
items.where: Drops from Soldier Construct IV in the Sky and the Depths.

items.name: Stalnox Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +29 attack; a strong heavy-weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from Stalnox (giant skeleton enemies) in caves and the Depths.

items.name: Star Fragment
items.effect: Fuse to a weapon/arrow for +1 attack and a burst of light (lights the area); on a shield it works as a lantern. A rare armor-upgrade material (used by the amiibo hero sets and at higher upgrade tiers), and added to a cooking pot it boosts the chance of a Critical Cook.
items.where: Look up at night — a falling star leaves a glowing landing spot; reach it before dawn to grab it. Also found in the Depths near certain mining spots and beneath some sky-island waterfalls.

items.name: Sundelion
items.effect: The gloom-cure material: cooked into a dish or elixir it restores 'gloom hearts' (the locked, darkened hearts from gloom damage). Can also be fused (+1 attack) but is far more valuable cooked.
items.where: Grows almost exclusively in the Sky — on the floating islands and on fallen sky-island debris on the surface; also dropped by Yiga foot soldiers/blademasters. Glows pale yellow.

items.name: Topaz
items.effect: Fuse Attack Power 1. On a weapon it shoots an electric orb; on an arrow it adds AOE electric damage (shocks and strips metal gear); on a shield a blocked attack sends out a wave of electricity. Used in armor upgrades (e.g. Topaz Earrings, Rubber set).
items.where: Break ore deposits (rare/golden deposits more likely) in caves and mountains; drops from Stone Talus variants; some shrine chests.

items.name: White Chuchu Jelly
items.effect: Fuse for +1 with a freezing effect; throw it to freeze water and foes.
items.where: Drops from Ice Chuchus in cold areas.

items.name: White-Maned Lynel Mace Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +40 attack; near-top two-handed weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from White-Maned Lynels.

items.name: White-Maned Lynel Saber Horn
items.effect: Fuse for +44 attack; near-top one-handed weapon fuse.
items.where: Drops from White-Maned Lynels (Faron/Central Hyrule Depths).

items.name: Yellow Chuchu Jelly
items.effect: Fuse for +1 with a shock effect; throw it to electrify water and metal foes.
items.where: Drops from Electric Chuchus.

items.name: Zonai Charge
items.effect: Consume from the menu to recover a small amount of your Energy Cell (the battery that powers Zonai devices). Can also be fused for +2 attack or cooked.
items.where: Dropped by Zonai constructs in the Sky and the Depths, and found in capsule dispensers and chests.

items.name: Zonaite
items.effect: The Depths' core resource: trade it to a Forge Construct for Crystallized Charges (battery/Energy Cell upgrades), and Autobuild spends it to recreate parts when none are nearby. Fuse gives only a minor +1 attack.
items.where: Mine Zonaite deposits throughout the Depths (and the Great Sky Island starter cave); also drops from Frox.

items.name: Ancient Arowana
items.effect: Lively fish; cooked it restores a little health with no special bonus (Sunny-compatible).
items.where: Lakes/ponds on Great Sky Island and North Tabantha Sky Archipelago.

items.name: Armored Carp
items.effect: Hard-scaled carp; cooked it gives a Tough bonus (Defense Up).
items.where: Lakes and rivers of East Necluda and Lanayru Great Spring.

items.name: Armored Porgy
items.effect: Armor-scaled sea fish; cooked it gives a Tough bonus (Defense Up).
items.where: The seas of Necluda and Lanayru.

items.name: Bladed Rhino Beetle
items.effect: Beetle clinging to rocks/cliffs. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Mighty elixir (Attack Up).
items.where: West Necluda and East Necluda, on rocks and cliffs; also dropped by Evermean.

items.name: Bright-Eyed Crab
items.effect: Crab; cooked it gives an Energizing bonus (restores stamina).
items.where: Shores of Zora's Domain, Lanayru Sea and Necluda Sea — appear while it is raining.

items.name: Chillfin Trout
items.effect: Blue trout; cooked it gives a Chilly bonus (heat resistance).
items.where: Rivers, lakes and cave streams of Hebra Mountains and Lanayru Great Spring.

items.name: Cold Darner
items.effect: Dragonfly from cold mountain passes. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Chilly elixir (heat resistance).
items.where: Cold mountain areas — Gerudo Highlands and Mount Lanayru. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Deep Firefly
items.effect: Underground firefly. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Bright elixir (glow effect); used to upgrade glow/Depths armor.
items.where: The Depths — e.g. the Great Abandoned Central Mine; also caves and Hyrule Field Chasm.

items.name: Electric Darner
items.effect: Dragonfly from storm-prone zones. Cooked with a monster part it makes an Electro elixir (shock resistance).
items.where: Rain/storm areas — Thunderhead Isles and West Necluda.

items.name: Energetic Rhino Beetle
items.effect: Rare beetle gathered at night. Cooked with a monster part it makes an Energizing elixir (greatly restores stamina); 3 per piece upgrade the Hero of the Wild set (Cap/Tunic/Trousers of the Wild).
items.where: Great Sky Island and West Necluda — on tree trunks at night when it isn't raining; also dropped by Evermean.

items.name: Fairy
items.effect: Holding one auto-revives Link (and restores ~5 hearts) when he runs out of health; can also be cooked into a Fairy Tonic.
items.where: Sky-island ponds, the Outskirt Stable well, and caves near Great Fairy fountains (e.g. near Mija); approach stealthily or grab while gliding.

items.name: Fireproof Lizard
items.effect: Heat-loving lizard. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Fireproof elixir (flame resistance) — the key non-armor way to make Fireproof Elixirs.
items.where: Eldin region — Death Mountain, Eldin Canyon, Eldin Mountains. Sold by Beedle for 25.

items.name: Glowing Cave Fish
items.effect: Luminous fish; cooked it gives a Glow/Bright bonus (makes Link glow); used to upgrade glow/Miner's armor.
items.where: Waters inside dark caves throughout Hyrule.

items.name: Hearty Bass
items.effect: Cooked into Hearty dishes — full hearts plus temporary bonus hearts; used to upgrade the Zora armor set.
items.where: Sky-island ponds — Tabantha Sky Archipelago and West Hebra Sky.

items.name: Hearty Lizard
items.effect: Lizard found near mountains. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Hearty elixir (full hearts plus temporary bonus hearts).
items.where: Gerudo Highlands and East Necluda; around several stables. Sold by Beedle for 100.

items.name: Hearty Salmon
items.effect: Cooked into Hearty dishes — full hearts plus temporary bonus hearts.
items.where: Rivers of cold regions — Hebra Mountains and Tabantha Frontier; sometimes dropped by bears.

items.name: Hightail Lizard
items.effect: Fast lizard. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Hasty elixir (movement-speed boost).
items.where: Under rocks and boulders in Hyrule Field and Hyrule Ridge. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Hot-Footed Frog
items.effect: Quick frog found near water. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Hasty elixir (movement-speed boost).
items.where: Near rivers/streams — Lanayru Great Spring and East Necluda. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Hyrule Bass
items.effect: Ordinary fish; cooked it restores hearts with no special bonus (Sunny-compatible). Used to upgrade the Zora armor set.
items.where: Lakes and rivers across Hyrule Field and West Necluda.

items.name: Ironshell Crab
items.effect: Hard-shelled crab; cooked it gives a Tough bonus (Defense Up).
items.where: Shores of the Faron Sea and Necluda Sea.

items.name: Mighty Carp
items.effect: Carp; cooked it gives a Mighty bonus (Attack Up).
items.where: Lakes and rivers of Lanayru Great Spring and Akkala Highlands.

items.name: Mighty Porgy
items.effect: Sea fish; cooked it gives a Mighty bonus (Attack Up).
items.where: The seas of Faron and Necluda.

items.name: Razorclaw Crab
items.effect: Crab; cooked it gives a Mighty bonus (Attack Up); used to upgrade attack/Barbarian-style armor.
items.where: Shores of the Lanayru Sea and Necluda Sea.

items.name: Restless Cricket
items.effect: Small jumping bug. Cooked with a monster part it makes an Energizing elixir (restores stamina).
items.where: Great Sky Island and Hyrule Field. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Rugged Rhino Beetle
items.effect: Beetle from forest tree trunks. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Tough elixir (Defense Up).
items.where: West and East Necluda, on the sides of forest trees; also dropped by Evermean.

items.name: Sanke Carp
items.effect: Decorative carp; cooked it restores hearts with no special bonus (Sunny-compatible).
items.where: Water around the chief's house in Kakariko Village.

items.name: Sizzlefin Trout
items.effect: Warm-water trout; cooked it gives a Spicy bonus (cold resistance).
items.where: Warm water / hot springs in Eldin Canyon and Hebra Mountains.

items.name: Smotherwing Butterfly
items.effect: Butterfly from hot volcanic zones. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Fireproof elixir (flame resistance).
items.where: Hot regions — Death Mountain and Eldin Canyon. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Sneaky River Snail
items.effect: Glow-in-the-dark snail; cooked it gives a Sneaky bonus (Stealth Up); used to upgrade stealth armor.
items.where: Shallow water near lakes/rivers — Zora's Domain (and the cave below it) and Hyrule Ridge.

items.name: Staminoka Bass
items.effect: Cooked into Energizing dishes — restores stamina.
items.where: Ponds and lakes of Hyrule Field and West Necluda (incl. Rito/Hebra ponds).

items.name: Stealthfin Trout
items.effect: Bioluminescent trout; cooked it gives a Sneaky bonus (Stealth Up).
items.where: Lakes and rivers of Great Hyrule Forest at night.

items.name: Sticky Frog
items.effect: Frog from wet walls. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Slip-Resistance (Sticky) elixir — climb wet/slick surfaces.
items.where: On cliffs and walls in wet areas — West Necluda and Lanayru Great Spring. Sold by Beedle for 40.

items.name: Sticky Lizard
items.effect: Cave lizard. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Slip-Resistance (Sticky) elixir — climb wet/slick surfaces.
items.where: Inside caves throughout Hyrule. Sold by Beedle for 32.

items.name: Summerwing Butterfly
items.effect: Warm-region butterfly. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Spicy elixir (cold resistance / warmth).
items.where: Warm regions — Eldin Canyon, Gerudo Highlands, Kara Kara Bazaar; sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Sunset Firefly
items.effect: Glowing bug active at night. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Sneaky elixir (Stealth Up); used to upgrade stealth armor.
items.where: Forests/grasslands at night — Faron Grasslands and East Necluda. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Thunderwing Butterfly
items.effect: Butterfly whose organs insulate. Cooked with a monster part it makes an Electro elixir (shock resistance).
items.where: Rainy/storm areas — Thunderhead Isles and Faron Grasslands; sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Tireless Frog
items.effect: Rare frog. Cooked with a monster part it makes an Enduring elixir (full stamina plus temporary extra stamina wheels).
items.where: Dark, moist spots near water — Hyrule Field and Zora River. Sold by Beedle for 100.

items.name: Voltfin Trout
items.effect: Trout with insulating scales; cooked it gives an Electro bonus (shock resistance); used to upgrade shock-resistance armor.
items.where: Tabantha Frontier and Hebra Mountains (e.g. Tama Pond / Hebra Plunge).

items.name: Warm Darner
items.effect: Dragonfly from warm climates. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Spicy elixir (cold resistance).
items.where: Warm areas — Akkala Highlands and Faron Grasslands. Sold by Beedle for 10.

items.name: Winterwing Butterfly
items.effect: Cold-region butterfly. Cooked with a monster part it makes a Chilly elixir (heat resistance). Also used to upgrade cold-resistance armor.
items.where: Cold areas — Gerudo Highlands and Mount Lanayru; around Tabantha Bridge Stable. Beedle sells it for 10.
